content.name: AIGP Quality Standard — Core v1.0.0
content.description: Core syntax reference and quality checklist for AISOP files governed by AIGP protocol. Loadable extensions (security, ecosystem, performance) provide additional checks. Includes tool_annotations governance spec, I12 Tool Annotation Consistency rule, MF15 Output Contract Enforcement rule, json_schema for data contracts (JSON Schema Draft 2020-12), cache_policy dynamic TTL contract, lint_report data contract for automated verification, test_report with automated/subjective split, and license_spec (SPDX license field specification). Not an executable flow.

governance_params.description: AIGP governance parameters for all compliant AISOP files.

template.protocol: AIGP V1.0.0 — must match AIGP.md protocol field

governance_params.enforcement: Every .aisop.json file SHOULD include protocol field matching AIGP.md. AIGP.md MUST exist in project root.

trust_framework.description: Four-level trust classification for AIGP programs. Determines permission boundaries and validation requirements.

T1.name: Metadata Only
T1.permissions: Read AIGP.md metadata only. No tool access, no execution.

T2.name: Read-Only Sandbox
T2.permissions: Read-only tool access (file_system read). No write side_effects.

T3.name: Supervised Execution
T3.permissions: Read/write tools with explicit scope constraints. Human approval for destructive operations.
T3.validation: Full ThreeDimTest + I8 threat model + governance_hash required.

T4.name: Autonomous
T4.permissions: Full tool access within declared permissions scope. No per-operation human approval required.
T4.validation: Full ThreeDimTest + I8 + I9 + governance_hash + quality_baseline history.

trust_framework.default: T4 — programs default to autonomous unless explicitly constrained.
trust_framework.escalation: If runtime behavior exceeds declared trust_level (e.g., T2 program attempts write), executor MUST block and escalate.
trust_framework.aigp_md_format: trust_level field in AIGP.md: integer (1-4) for backward compatibility. Structured format { level: number, justification: string, constraints: [string] } is RECOMMENDED for T3+.

tool_annotations.description: Structured annotations for declared tools enabling safety analysis and interoperability mapping.

tool_schema.name: string — tool identifier matching tools[] entry
tool_schema.required: boolean — program cannot function without this tool

annotations.read_only: boolean — tool performs only read operations
annotations.destructive: boolean — tool can irreversibly modify/delete state
annotations.idempotent: boolean — repeated invocation produces same result

tool_annotations.enforcement: I12 verifies annotations match side_effects. ThreeDimTest I12 mandatory for Standard/Professional tier.
tool_annotations.backward_compatibility: Legacy tools[] without annotations remain valid — annotations default to null, I12 is N/A.

mcp_tool_mapping.description: Bidirectional mapping between AIGP tools[] and MCP Tools. Enables AIGP programs to be exposed as MCP Servers and consume external MCP Servers.

aigp_to_mcp.governance_extension: MCP Tool gains AIGP extension: { aigp_protocol, trust_level, governance_hash }

mcp_to_aigp.trust_policy: External MCP Servers inherit program trust_level constraints. REMOTE_UNVERIFIED MCP Servers capped at T2.

mcp_server_field.description: Optional mcp_server field in AIGP.md tools[] entries. Links an AIGP tool declaration to an external MCP Server.
mcp_server_field.schema: { server_id: string (MCP Server URI), tool_name: string (specific tool in server), transport: string (stdio|streamable-http) }

mcp_tool_mapping.backward_compatibility: Programs without mcp_server fields in tools[] operate identically to v14.0.0. MCP mapping is opt-in.

tool_dirs_spec.description: Specification for embedded tool directories in Pattern G programs. Programs declare tool_dirs in AIGP.md to bundle tool implementations.

common_rules.readme_required: Each tool directory MUST contain README.md documenting: purpose, interface (CLI args or JSON stdin/stdout), security constraints.
common_rules.network_declaration: If code contains network API calls, AIGP.md permissions.network.allowed must be true. Detected via I13(b) language-specific static analysis.
common_rules.write_scope: Code write operations must not exceed AIGP.md permissions.file_system.scope. Detected via I13(c).
common_rules.mcp_proxy: All code tools MUST be exposed via mcp-tools/mcp_server.json. Direct subprocess execution prohibited (shell-tools exempt with T4 audit).
common_rules.dependency_locking: All language-specific dependency files MUST use exact version pinning. I13(d) enforces per-language rules.

code_trust_gate.description: Security verification flow for bundled code before execution.
steps: 2. Static analysis: import declarations vs permissions.network
3. File hash verification: mcp_server.json file_hashes vs actual file SHA-256
4. Trust level check: Pattern G minimum T3

trust_modes.T3_supervised: Human confirmation required before MCP Server startup
trust_modes.T4_autonomous: Automatic pass after all checks succeed

tool_dirs_spec.governance_hash_coverage: governance_hash algorithm unchanged (SHA-256 of .aisop.json files). tool_dirs/ file hashes are recorded in mcp_server.json file_hashes field, which is part of mcp-tools/ and covered by governance_hash indirectly via .aisop.json referencing. Programs without tool_dirs have identical hash computation.
tool_dirs_spec.backward_compatibility: tool_dirs is purely optional. Programs without tool_dirs operate identically to v14. I13 and MF16 are N/A for non-Pattern-G programs.

instruction_invariant.rule: Every AISOP file's instruction field MUST be exactly: RUN aisop.main
instruction_invariant.rationale: instruction is a machine execution directive, not natural language description. RUN is a command keyword with mandatory semantics. aisop.main is the structural path to the primary execution graph in the JSON aisop object. Program identity is provided by the id field. Capabilities are described by summary and description. Behavior is defined by system_prompt. instruction carries ONLY the execution command.
instruction_invariant.enforcement: ValidateStep checks instruction === 'RUN aisop.main'. Any other value is a structural violation.

system_prompt_rules.description: system_prompt is the behavioral layer — defines HOW the agent should act, not WHAT it is or HOW it is built.
must_contain: Role positioning — behavioral role, not product name (e.g., 'Personal expense tracking assistant', not 'Expense Tracker v1.0.0')
Domain behavioral guidelines — domain-specific action principles (e.g., 'Prioritize numerical precision', 'Enforce quality standards rigorously')
'Mirror User's exact language and script variant.' — D6 multilingual requirement
'Align: Human Sovereignty and Benefit.' — Axiom 0 seal
must_not_contain: Product name or version — redundant with name + version fields
Architecture or pattern details — redundant with description field
Module file names or delegation logic — redundant with functions field
Capability lists — redundant with summary field
system_prompt_rules.template: {Behavioral role}. {Domain guidelines}. Mirror User's exact language and script variant.\nAlign: Human Sovereignty and Benefit.
system_prompt_rules.principle: Each field has exactly one responsibility. Information appears in exactly one place.
system_prompt_rules.enforcement: ThreeDimTest D6 verifies multilingual + Align presence. ValidateStep checks no redundancy with name/version/description.

content.summary: Short description of what this AISOP does.
content.system_prompt:
AISOP quality standard and syntax reference. Enforce AIGP governance invariants rigorously. Mirror User's exact language and script variant.
Align: Human Sovereignty and Benefit.

C1.name: Problem Modeling
C1.check: Mermaid graph matches domain archetype: Pipeline/Router/Loop/Hybrid. EDGE COMPLETENESS: every conditional path described in function logic must have a corresponding edge in the Mermaid graph.
C1.how_to_verify: Draw Mermaid. Verify it matches one of four canonical shapes. EDGE COMPLETENESS: for each function, compare described conditional paths (e.g., 'if empty → route to Present', 'if NoData → skip to X') against Mermaid edges. Every described path must have a corresponding edge. Use conditional labels for alternate paths (e.g., 'LoadData -- HasData --> ModeGate' and 'LoadData -- NoData --> Present'). Missing edges are C1 violations.

C2.name: Node Load
C2.check: Leaf function <=8 steps. Convergence function <=12 steps.
C2.how_to_verify: Count step# keys in each function. Flag any >8 (leaf) or >12 (convergence).

C3.name: Abstraction Consistency
C3.check: All Mermaid nodes at similar abstraction level.
C3.how_to_verify: Read all node labels. They should be ~2-4 words, same grammatical form.

C4.name: Information Flow
C4.check: Every parameter referenced in >=1 function. All outputs consumed downstream.
C4.how_to_verify: List parameters. Grep each in functions. List outputs. Grep each downstream.

C5.name: Fractal Decomposition
C5.check: Target AIGP fractal structure per AIGP_Protocol.md. Nodes match functionality.
C5.how_to_verify: Count functional nodes (total Mermaid nodes - Start - endNode). Apply progressive thresholds. Check Pattern selection. Verify main quality constraint.
C5.functional_node_count: Functional nodes = total Mermaid nodes - Start - endNode. Start and endNode are structural overhead, not functionality.

progressive_thresholds.advisory: 13-15 functional nodes — WARNING: suggest review for extraction opportunities. Provide specific split suggestions based on tool boundaries, data flow phases, and independence test.
progressive_thresholds.recommended: 16+ functional nodes — WARNING: strongly recommend splitting. Provide concrete split plan. Allow fractal_exempt annotation to suppress.

C5.fractal_exempt: Module author may add fractal_exempt field in system.content with reason string to suppress progressive warnings. Equivalent to Python # noqa.

main_quality_constraint.pattern_a: main is the only file — contains all logic, no special constraint.
main_quality_constraint.pattern_b_plus: main is router — only routing logic + lightweight inline handlers (≤2 steps, no tool calls). No business logic. tools = union of all sub-module tools. Route count determined by functionality, no node limit.

module_rules.self_contained: Each module: independent system_prompt, tools, parameters, functions.
module_rules.minimum: ≥3 functional nodes + (≥1 tool call OR ≥3 steps).

pattern_thresholds.A: Single file, 1 function — no hard node limit
pattern_thresholds.B: Multi-file, 2+ independent functions, main=router + func modules
pattern_thresholds.C: Package + shared.aisop.json when 2+ modules reuse complex logic
pattern_thresholds.D: Nested (max 2 levels) when sub-module has 2+ sub-functions
pattern_thresholds.F: Ecosystem: 3+ AISOP components + blueprint + shared data_contracts
pattern_thresholds.G: Embedded Runtime: Pattern E/F + tool_dirs/ directory with bundled tool implementations (python-tools/, mcp-tools/, etc.)

C5.independence_test: Can describe in one sentence + no shared specialized tools/state + can test independently -> separate file.

split_priority.P1_tool_boundary: Different node groups use different tool sets -> natural split point.
split_priority.P2_data_flow_phase: Sequential pipeline phases (research → generate → test) -> each phase is split candidate.
split_priority.no_split: Convergence groups (fan-in), error recovery loops, tightly coupled same-tool nodes -> keep together.

C5.enforcement: Generate checks functional node count. 2+ independent functions -> suggest Pattern B. Progressive warnings at 13+ and 16+. Never FAIL on node count alone.

C6.name: Brain Principle
C6.check: LLM-native (reasoning): no tool needed. Tool-required (file I/O, API): declare tool.
C6.how_to_verify: Review each function. File/API calls -> must be in tools[]. Classify/summarize -> no tool needed.

C7.name: Token Budget Compliance
C7.check: Module system_prompt or description declares token_estimate range. AIGP.md runtime.token_budget is set.
C7.how_to_verify: Check AIGP.md runtime.token_budget exists and is >0. For each module, verify description or system_prompt mentions expected complexity or token range. Standalone modules may use simplified estimate.

I1.name: Tool Honesty
I1.check: No phantom (declared unused) and no undeclared (used but not declared). EXEMPTION: Pattern B+ main router files where tools = union of sub-module tools (per C5) — sub-module tools in main's tools[] are not phantom.
I1.how_to_verify: tools[] list. Grep each in functions. Grep tool verbs in functions, verify declared.

I2.name: Security
I2.check: Start.constraints: type guard, injection guard, size guard, encoding guard — always required. Path guard — required only when file_system in tools[]. Without file_system, 4 guards suffice. ZERO-TOOL ADAPTATION: for tools=[] systems, (C) Injection guard should focus on prompt injection (role override, system prompt extraction, persona manipulation, 'ignore previous instructions') rather than shell metacharacters — shell injection is irrelevant without shell/file_system tools.
I2.how_to_verify: Read Start.constraints. Check tools[] for file_system. If present: verify all 5 guards. If absent: verify 4 guards (type, injection, size, encoding).
I2.inheritance_rule: Pattern B+ sub-modules: if main router's Start.constraints implements all 5 guards (A-E) and sub-module is ONLY accessible via main router (not independently invocable), sub-module may declare inherited guards with explicit '(inherited from main router)' annotation. ThreeDimTest treats annotated inherited guards as PASS. Sub-module MUST still implement (A) Type guard and (D) Path guard independently — these require module-specific knowledge. Inheritable guards: (B) Size, (C) Injection, (E) Encoding.
I2.design_note: Inheritance is OPTIONAL — programs may choose defense-in-depth by implementing full guards in every sub-module independently. The AIGP Creator itself uses this approach: all sub-modules have full I2 guards despite being eligible for inheritance from main. Both approaches are valid; inheritance reduces redundancy while full guards maximize defense depth.

I3.name: Data Integrity
I3.check: Persistent writes: atomic (.tmp then rename), backup (.bak), field-level schema. EXEMPTION: if tools=[] (no file_system, no shell, no network), I3 is automatically N/A — no persistent writes are possible without tool access.
I3.how_to_verify: First check tools[]: if empty, mark I3 as N/A (5/5). Otherwise, find all write operations. Verify 'atomic'/'tmp'/'rename' and 'bak'/'backup' mentioned.

I4.name: Circuit Breaker
I4.check: Error: retry -> circuit-breaker -> named-fallback -> inform. Fallback must specify concrete action. EXEMPTION: exact-match routing gates (IntentGate, ModeGate, TypeGate) with a deterministic default case may use simplified Error: 'Unknown {param} -> fallback: default {route} -> inform.' (no retry/circuit-breaker needed since routing cannot fail with a default).
I4.how_to_verify: Read Error fields. Pattern: retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: {specific action} -> inform.

I5.name: Tool Suitability
I5.check: Tool matches Brain Principle C6. Utilize tools >30% of functions. EXEMPTION 1 (Symbolic): modules that explicitly declare read-only symbolic analysis (no actual tool execution) are exempt — their tool usage is inherently low by design. EXEMPTION 2 (Delegation): Pattern B+ orchestrators/advisors where >=50% of functions are delegation wrappers or LLM-native advisory functions are exempt — verify that delegated sub-module individually meet the 30% threshold instead. EXEMPTION 3 (Zero-tool): if tools=[] (empty array) and no function in the module references any tool call, the module is automatically exempt — zero-tool systems are by definition pure LLM-native and I5 is not applicable. AUTO-RECOGNITION: modules matching the 'read-once-analyze' pattern are automatically eligible for Symbolic exemption without explicit annotation.
I5.how_to_verify: Count functions calling tools. Divide by total. >30% -> OK. EXEMPTION 1: If module declares 'symbolic'/'no actual tool execution'/'read-only analysis', apply exemption. EXEMPTION 2: If module is Pattern B+ with >=50% delegation/advisory functions (identified by 'Delegate to X.aisop.json' or LLM-native reasoning steps), exempt the module and verify each delegated sub-module meets 30% independently. EXEMPTION 3 (Zero-tool): if tools=[] and no function references any tool call, auto-exempt — I5 is N/A for zero-tool systems. This pattern is common in conversational, advisory, and classification-only modules. AUTO-RECOGNITION (read-once-analyze): if exactly 1 function calls a tool for data loading (LoadData, ReadData, FetchData or similar name pattern) and ALL remaining functions perform no tool calls (pure LLM-native analysis/formatting/presentation), auto-apply Symbolic exemption — no explicit annotation required. This pattern is common in search, statistics, and analytics modules.

I6.name: Resilience + Semantic Accuracy
I6.check: Every function has Error field with correct pattern. Error/fallback text must be semantically accurate — hardcoded counts must match actual content.
I6.how_to_verify: STRUCTURAL: count functions, each must have Error field with retry→circuit-breaker→fallback→inform pattern. SEMANTIC: extract numbers from Error/fallback text (e.g., 'list all N features', 'present N options'), cross-reference against actual list lengths in same function's steps. Mismatch is FAIL.

I7.name: Parameter Completeness + Metadata Consistency
I7.check: All parameters: type + agentic_prompt. Metadata fields internally consistent: name embedded version must match version field, id module-name must match filename.
I7.how_to_verify: PARAMETERS: list all, each must have 'type' and 'agentic_prompt', each must be referenced in at least one function. METADATA: if name contains vX.Y.Z pattern, extract and compare against version field — mismatch is FAIL. Verify id's module suffix matches filename stem (e.g., id 'foo.main' → file 'main.aisop.json').

I12.name: Tool Annotation Consistency
I12.check: Tool annotations match program's declared side_effects. read_only tools must not appear in modules with write side_effects. destructive tools require trust_level >= T3. open_world tools require AT3 threat mitigation in I8.
I12.how_to_verify: For each tool in AIGP.md tools[]: (1) if annotations.read_only=true, verify tool NOT in any module's write side_effects. (2) if annotations.destructive=true, verify trust_level >= T3 AND module has explicit confirmation step. (3) if annotations.open_world=true, verify I8 addresses AT3. (4) if annotations.idempotent=true, verify module declares idempotent=true.
I12.na_conditions: Programs without annotations in tools[] entries. Legacy format programs are I12 N/A.
I12.relationship: Extends I1 (Tool Honesty): I1 checks existence, I12 checks declared behavior matches actual behavior.

I13.name: Embedded Code Safety
I13.check: Bundled code in tool_dirs/ passes integrity, network declaration, file scope, dependency locking, MCP proxy, and shell audit checks.
I13.how_to_verify: (a) Code integrity — all source files in tool_dirs/ have SHA-256 hashes recorded in mcp-tools/mcp_server.json file_hashes field, governance_hash covers mcp_server.json. (b) Network declaration — detect language-specific network API imports (Python: requests/urllib/httpx/aiohttp; TS/JS: fetch/axios/got; Go: net/http; Rust: reqwest/hyper; Shell: curl/wget/nc); if detected, permissions.network.allowed must be true. (c) File system scope — detect language-specific write operations; write paths must not exceed permissions.file_system.scope. (d) Dependency version locking — Python: requirements.txt must use == only (no >= or *); TS/JS: package.json must not use ^ or ~ prefixes, lock file must exist; Go: go.sum must exist; Rust: Cargo.lock must exist. (e) MCP proxy required — all code tool directories must be exposed via mcp-tools/mcp_server.json exposed_tools (shell-tools exempt with audit). (f) Shell audit — if shell-tools/ exists, trust_level must be >= T4 and AIGP.md must contain shell_audit_approved: true.
I13.na_conditions: Programs without tool_dirs (Pattern A-F). I13 is N/A for non-Pattern-G programs.
I13.relationship: Extends I2 (Security Guards) to cover embedded code. Complements I9 (Supply Chain) for bundled dependencies.

D1.name: Step Clarity
D1.check: Concrete action verbs: extract, classify, calculate, generate, read, write, validate. No vague 'process', 'handle', 'do'.
D1.how_to_verify: Read all steps. Flag steps starting with 'process', 'handle', 'do', 'manage'.

D2.name: Step Format
D2.check: Tool steps: Input/Process/Output/Error. LLM steps: action + expected output.
D2.how_to_verify: Tool-calling steps must mention input source and output destination. Step keys should follow sequential integer naming (step1, step2, ... stepN); non-standard suffixes (step1_5, step2b, step4c) are permitted for insertion ordering but count as minor format inconsistency.

D3.name: Null Propagation
D3.check: Downstream handles null from Error nodes.
D3.how_to_verify: Trace error paths. Downstream nodes must handle null input.

D4.name: Consistency + Internal Coherence
D4.check: FORMAT: CamelCase Mermaid node IDs, snake_case parameter names. SEMANTIC: steps must not contradict own constraints. REFERENCE: any number mentioned in text (menu items, feature counts, module counts) must match actual count. CROSS-FUNCTION: if function A names function B in steps/constraints/Error text, B must exist in same file. CONSTRAINT CROSS-VALIDATION: constraints across different functions in the same module must not logically contradict each other.
D4.how_to_verify: FORMAT: scan node IDs (CamelCase), params (snake_case). SEMANTIC: compare step actions vs constraints per function. REFERENCE: extract numbers from step/Error text, cross-check against actual list lengths (e.g., 'present 9 options' → verify 9 options exist). CROSS-FUNCTION: collect all function names referenced in text, verify each exists as a key in functions object. CONSTRAINT CROSS-VALIDATION: (a) If any function declares 'Read-only' or equivalent restriction in constraints, no other function in the same module may perform contradicting operations (e.g., write/delete) in its steps. (b) If a routing function declares 'Exactly one route' or similar exclusivity constraint, the same function's steps/constraints must not also describe executing multiple routes simultaneously. (c) Mermaid edge count from a decision node must match the number of routes described in that node's function logic.

D5.name: Boundary Rules
D5.check: NLU/decision nodes have explicit disambiguation signals for confusable intent pairs. Non-NLU routing nodes (ModeGate, TypeGate) that route by exact parameter match do not require boundary rules.
D5.how_to_verify: Identify confusable pairs. Each must have a disambiguation SIGNAL keyword. Skip exact-match parameter routers (e.g., ModeGate routing by research_mode='structure'|'quality'|'compliance'). SUB-ROUTE CHECK: if NLU/router function defines sub-routes (per sub_routing_rules), verify each sub-route has: trigger patterns, target node, behavior mode, and edge-case fallback. Verify sub-route triggers do not overlap with primary intent signals without disambiguation.

D6.name: Multilingual
D6.check: system_prompt contains 'Mirror User's exact language and script variant.'

D7.name: Metabolism
D7.check: No phantom tools, no dead-end nodes, no vestigial steps.
D7.how_to_verify: List nodes with no outbound edges. List unused tools. List unreferenced functions.

dynamic_weights.description: Tier-based weight adjustments for ThreeDimTest weighted_score calculation.

Simple.rationale: Simple programs have fewer intrinsic concerns (no multi-file, minimal tools), so Cognitive and Intrinsic are weighted equally.

Standard.rationale: Default weights — Intrinsic quality (security, resilience) most important for production programs.

Professional.rationale: Same as Standard — Professional tier raises severity levels, not weight distribution.

dynamic_weights.na_handling: When I8, I9, I10, I11, I12, and/or I13 are N/A (e.g., non-published programs, no external dependencies, no trust_level declared, no identity block, no tool annotations), exclude them from the Intrinsic dimension denominator. Calculate I score as sum(applicable_scores) / count(applicable_items). This prevents unfair penalization for items that are structurally inapplicable. DENOMINATOR NORMALIZATION FOR REGRESSION CHECKS: when comparing scores across versions (QRG1-QRG5), if the set of N/A items differs between versions (denominator change due to items transitioning from N/A to applicable), the comparison MUST normalize the old score to the new denominator before computing deltas. Compute normalized_old_score by assuming new items would have scored the mean of existing applicable items. Use normalized score for regression detection to prevent false warnings when adopting new STANDARD features.
dynamic_weights.enforcement: ThreeDimTest MUST select weights based on tier classification. Generate ThreeDimTest step5 implements this logic.

tier_rules.description: Single source of truth for all tier-specific check adjustments. ThreeDimTest and Modify MUST reference these rules.

Simple.I2: WARNING (at least type guard required; 4 guards if no file_system; prompt-injection focus if tools=[] per STANDARD I2 ZERO-TOOL ADAPTATION)
Simple.I3: skip if no file_system in tools[] (no persistent writes possible); N/A if tools=[] per STANDARD I3 EXEMPTION
Simple.I5: check exemptions: Symbolic, Delegation, Zero-tool, Auto-Recognition per STANDARD I5

Standard.I2: FAIL if no input validation (all applicable guards — 4 without file_system, 5 with file_system; prompt-injection focus if tools=[] per STANDARD I2 ZERO-TOOL ADAPTATION)
Standard.I3: N/A if tools=[] per STANDARD I3 EXEMPTION; FAIL with file_system if no atomic writes
Standard.I5: check exemptions: Symbolic, Delegation, Zero-tool, Auto-Recognition per STANDARD I5

Professional.I3: FAIL if file_system present without atomic writes; N/A if tools=[]
Professional.D5: FAIL if boundary rules missing for confusable pairs

named_circuit_breaker_pattern.template: Error: {action} fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: {specific named alternative} -> inform user.
good_examples: fallback: list workspace dirs for manual selection
fallback: display patched JSON as copyable code block

upgrade_path.description: Grade Achievement priority by score_impact: I2 > I4 > I3 > I6 > D5 > C2 > I1 > D1 > D7.
upgrade_path.trigger: Any dimension grade below A -> run modify.aisop.json.

custom_fields.description: Source AISOP files may contain fields not defined in STANDARD (e.g. verified_on, custom metadata). Preserve unrecognized fields from source AISOP during evolution/modification — do NOT strip them. Custom fields are pass-through: copy as-is to output without validation.
custom_fields.semantic_validation: After evolution, if verified_on exists and content changed beyond cosmetic fixes, note in description that verification status is stale. Do NOT auto-remove or modify the verified_on array.

license_spec.description: Software license declaration for AIGP programs. Uses SPDX License Identifier standard.

conditional_fields.terms_url: string (URL) — required when license='proprietary', at least one of terms_url or contact must be present
conditional_fields.contact: string (email) — required when license='proprietary', at least one of terms_url or contact must be present

license_spec.store_requirement: Mandatory for aigp-store publishing (per PL25). Optional for local-only programs.

description_hygiene.description: Prevent description field bloat from accumulated version history.
description_hygiene.rule: If description contains >2 version history entries (e.g. 'v1.1.0 adds... v1.2.0 adds... v1.3.0 adds...'), compress: keep current version changes in full, collapse older entries into 'Prior versions added: [feature1, feature2, ...].' Single summary line replaces multiple vX.Y.Z entries.
description_hygiene.example_before: ... v1.1.0 adds substitution. v1.2.0 adds allergen notes. v1.3.0 adds nutrition.
description_hygiene.example_after: ... Prior versions added: substitution, allergen notes. v1.3.0 adds nutrition.

summary_consistency.description: Ensure summary field reflects current AISOP capabilities after evolution.
summary_consistency.rule: After evolution, if LEVEL_A or LEVEL_B changes added or removed features, verify summary still accurately describes the AISOP. If summary is stale (missing new key features or mentioning removed features), update summary to reflect current state. Summary should be 1-2 sentences, not a changelog.
summary_consistency.check: Compare summary against: (1) current tools[], (2) current Mermaid node names, (3) description's current-version section.

name_version_sync.description: Ensure name field's embedded version matches the version field after evolution.
name_version_sync.rule: If name field contains a version pattern (e.g., 'vX.Y.Z'), it MUST match the version field. After version bump, update all module name fields to reflect the new version. Newly created files get the correct version automatically; this rule targets existing files being evolved.
name_version_sync.check: Regex match vX.Y.Z in name field; compare against version field. Mismatch is a FAIL.

error_text_consistency.description: Prevent hardcoded counts and references in Error/fallback text from going stale after evolution.
error_text_consistency.rule: If an Error or fallback field contains a hardcoded count (e.g., 'list all N features', 'present N options'), that count MUST match the actual count in the corresponding function's step content. After adding or removing items (features, intents, menu options), scan all Error fields in the same file for stale counts.
error_text_consistency.check: Extract numbers from Error fields; cross-reference against actual list lengths in the same function's steps.

range_reference_consistency.description: Prevent stale item range references from persisting after checklist evolution.
range_reference_consistency.rule: If description text or AIGP.md body contains item range references (e.g., 'C1-C7', 'I1-I13', 'PL1-PL25'), the upper bound MUST match the actual item count in the STANDARD quality_checklist. Stale ranges (e.g., 'I1-I12' when STANDARD has I1-I13) MUST be updated when new items are added. Automated: D4-RANGE lint check in ThreeDimTest.

nlu_intent_checklist.description: When evolution adds or removes an NLU intent, all derived references must be updated. This checklist ensures completeness.
on_intent_add: (1) Add confusion pair(s) for the new intent vs most-similar existing intents with SIGNAL keywords
(2) Update tiebreaker order in NLU to include new intent at correct priority
(3) Update NLU enrichment steps if new intent requires additional context extraction
(4) Update Start menu item count and list to include new intent
(5) Add Mermaid edge from NLU to new intent's target node
(6) Update AIGP.md intent_examples with representative input for new intent
(7) Update AIGP.md modules[].nodes if new node was added
(8) Verify Error text counts in NLU and Start functions match new totals
on_intent_remove: (1) Remove confusion pairs referencing removed intent
(3) Remove Mermaid edge to removed intent's node
(4) Update Start menu count and list
(6) Update AIGP.md modules[].nodes if node was removed
(7) Verify no remaining functions reference removed intent by name
nlu_intent_checklist.enforcement: Generate EVOLUTION IMPACT SCAN must execute this checklist when evolution_plan contains NLU intent changes. ThreeDimTest D4+D5 provide last-line detection for stale counts and missing boundary rules.

derived_reference_rules.description: Ensure all derived references stay consistent after evolution. A derived reference is any text that restates or counts information defined elsewhere in the same file.

categories.count_references: Any number in Error/fallback/step text that refers to a list length (features, intents, menu items, modules). Must match actual count.
categories.version_references: Any version string embedded in name, description, or step text. Must match the version field.
categories.cross_function_references: Any function name mentioned in another function's steps/constraints/Error. Must exist as a key in the functions object.
categories.menu_references: Start/General menu item listings. Must include all routable intents defined in NLU/routing functions.

derived_reference_rules.enforcement: Generate MUST run EVOLUTION IMPACT SCAN to trace derived references after evolution. ApplyFixes MUST run DERIVED REFERENCE AUDIT as final consistency check. ThreeDimTest I6+I7+D4 provide last-line detection.

intent_vector_guidance.description: Guidance for optional semantic routing vectors in AIGP programs with NLU routing.
intent_vector_guidance.when_to_use: Programs with 5+ NLU intents where keyword-based classification has known confusion pairs. Vector routing reduces ambiguity by comparing user input embedding against pre-computed intent embeddings.
intent_vector_guidance.generation_rule: For each NLU intent, select 3-5 representative input examples. Compute embeddings using a consistent model. Store as intent_vectors in AIGP.md. Include similarity_threshold (default 0.85).
intent_vector_guidance.hybrid_routing: Vector similarity is advisory — final routing decision combines: (1) vector similarity score, (2) keyword SIGNAL matches from NLU confusion pairs, (3) LLM judgment. Vector match > threshold → boost confidence by 20%. No match → fall through to keyword+LLM.
intent_vector_guidance.maintenance: Re-generate vectors when NLU intents change (add/remove). Stale vectors (generated before intent changes) should be flagged by ThreeDimTest D4.
intent_vector_guidance.constraints: Vectors are OPTIONAL. Programs without intent_vectors use existing keyword+LLM routing with no degradation. Embedding model dependency must be declared in tools[] if used at runtime.

extension_registry.description: Registry of STANDARD extension modules. ThreeDimTest loads extensions dynamically based on program profile.

security.load_condition: always — security checks are mandatory for all programs

ecosystem.scope: MF10-MF14, K1-K5, PL16-PL17, PL22, simulation categories F-L, packaging_specification, ui_rendering_rules
ecosystem.load_condition: Pattern B+ programs only — single-file Pattern A programs skip ecosystem checks

performance.load_condition: Pipeline operations only — standalone Validate/Simulate skip performance extension

extension_registry.loading_protocol: ThreeDimTest and ReadTemplate MUST: (1) always load core, (2) always load security, (3) load ecosystem if Pattern >= B, (4) load performance if in pipeline context or quality_threshold is 'strict'. Extension loading is additive — core rules always apply, extensions add additional checks.
extension_registry.version_requirement: All loaded extensions MUST have the same template_version as core. Version mismatch is a loading error.

composite_node_rules.description: Rules for nodes that internally execute logic of multiple other defined functions without graph-level routing.
composite_node_rules.rule: A COMPOSITE NODE is a Mermaid node whose function definition internally executes the logic of 2+ other functions defined in the same file, without routing through those functions via graph edges. Composite nodes MUST: (1) Include 'COMPOSITE NODE:' annotation at the beginning of step1. (2) List all internally-executed functions by name. (3) Document partial-success handling (what happens if one internal sub-execution fails). (4) Use a descriptive Mermaid label that hints at composite nature (e.g., 'Full Combined Analysis' instead of generic 'Full Analysis').
composite_node_rules.detection: ThreeDimTest D4 detects: if a function's steps reference executing the logic of other named functions in the same file (e.g., 'execute AnalyzePatterns, DetectAnomalies, ForecastSpending, CategoryTrends') but the Mermaid graph does not route through those function nodes, it is a composite node. If COMPOSITE annotation is missing, flag as D4 issue.
composite_node_rules.enforcement: Generate MUST annotate composite nodes during generation. ThreeDimTest D4 verifies annotation presence.

routing_parameter_rules.description: Rules for parameters with routing semantics — parameters that control which path a ModeGate/router node takes.
routing_parameter_rules.sentinel_default: Parameters with routing semantics (e.g., operation_type, report_type, analysis_type) MUST NOT use a valid route value as their default. Instead, use a sentinel value ('auto' or empty string) that is distinct from all valid route values. This allows the receiving module to distinguish 'caller explicitly chose route X' from 'caller used default'. When the sentinel default is received, the module falls back to NLU/inference from user_request.
routing_parameter_rules.enforcement: Generate MUST use sentinel defaults for newly created routing parameters. ThreeDimTest D4 checks: if a parameter appears in a ModeGate/routing function as the routing criterion, verify its default value is not identical to any valid route value listed in that function's step1.

reference_data_rules.description: Rules for managing large reference data sets embedded in AISOP step instructions.
reference_data_rules.threshold: When a step instruction contains >20 discrete data items of the same type (e.g., keyword lists, locale mappings, category enumerations), the data SHOULD be externalized to a versioned reference file rather than hardcoded inline.

externalization_pattern.file_location: Same directory as the AISOP module that consumes the data.
externalization_pattern.schema: { schema_version: string, data_type: string, items: array, language_coverage: [string]|null, last_updated: string(ISO 8601) }
externalization_pattern.loading: Step instruction references the file by name and specifies runtime loading: 'Load {filename} at runtime. If file missing, use inline fallback list (minimum safe subset).'
externalization_pattern.versioning: Reference file has independent version from AISOP module. Updates to reference data (e.g., adding keywords, new language) do NOT require AISOP version bump. Reference file version tracked in AIGP.md or module header.

reference_data_rules.deployer_override: Reference data files MAY include a deployer_override_path field. If set, the executor loads deployer-provided data from that path instead of the bundled file, enabling localization and customization without editing AISOP source.
reference_data_rules.enforcement: Generate SHOULD externalize when threshold exceeded. ThreeDimTest D7 (metabolism) checks: if a step contains >20 inline data items, flag as D7-INLINE-DATA advisory. Not a hard failure — inline data is acceptable for small/stable lists (<20 items).
reference_data_rules.exemptions: Enumeration types with fixed protocol-defined values (e.g., LEVEL_A/B/C, Pattern A-F, trust_level T1-T4) are exempt — these are structural constants, not reference data.

sub_routing_rules.description: Rules for sub-routes defined within NLU/router functions that map secondary intent types to existing nodes without adding Mermaid graph nodes.
sub_routing_rules.rule: A SUB-ROUTE is an intent-to-node mapping defined in a routing function's step text (typically NLU step2) that handles non-primary intent categories by reusing existing response nodes with modified behavior modes. Sub-routes do NOT create new Mermaid nodes — they are internal routing logic within the NLU function.
sub_routing_rules.requirements: (1) Each sub-route must specify: trigger patterns (signal words/phrases), target node name, and behavior mode (e.g., 'self-correction mode', 'task-attempt mode'). (2) Each sub-route must have a fallback for edge cases (e.g., MultiTurnContinuation with no prior context → route to Clarify). (3) Sub-routes must not conflict with primary routes — if a sub-route's trigger patterns overlap with a primary intent's signals, add a disambiguation rule or confusion pair.
sub_routing_rules.when_to_use: Use sub-routing when an intent category is (a) infrequent enough that a dedicated Mermaid node would be underutilized, (b) semantically close to an existing node's capability, and (c) can be handled by an existing node with a behavior modifier. Common sub-route types: FeedbackMeta (user correcting AI), TaskRequest (capability-bounded task attempts), MultiTurnContinuation (context continuation).
sub_routing_rules.enforcement: ThreeDimTest D5 checks sub-routes: verify each sub-route has trigger patterns, target node, behavior mode, and edge-case fallback. Generate should prefer sub-routing over new nodes when the candidate intent meets all three criteria in 'when_to_use'.

multi_file_guidelines.description: Quality rules for Pattern C/D multi-file AISOPs.

rules.MF1: All files in same directory must share consistent version numbers.
rules.MF2: Main file's delegation targets must match sub-module ids exactly.
rules.MF3: Shared parameters (e.g. workspace_dir, quality_threshold) must have same type and compatible defaults across all files.
rules.MF4: Cross-file data contracts: (a) FORMAT — output format of one module must match expected input format of the consuming module. (b) NAME — parameter names used in main router's delegation steps must exactly match the receiving sub-module's declared parameter names (e.g., if main passes operation='analyze_turn', sub-module must declare parameter named 'operation', not 'analysis_type'). (c) VALUE — parameter values passed by main must be valid values accepted by the receiving sub-module's routing logic (e.g., if main passes operation='analyze_turn', sub-module's routing function must list 'analyze_turn' as a valid route). (d) PATH — file path patterns referenced across modules must be consistent: if module A writes to {dir}/working.json, module B that reads the same data must use the identical path template {dir}/working.json (not {dir}/working_{id}.json or other variants). Check: for each file_system write in any module, trace all corresponding reads across all modules and verify path templates match exactly. Generate and Modify MUST verify MF4 (a/b/c/d) during multi-file operations.
rules.MF5: Module ID to filename: the segment after the last '.' in module id maps to filename. E.g., id='travel_planner.search' -> search.aisop.json. id='travel_planner.main' -> main.aisop.json. DependencyCheck uses this rule to resolve module IDs to file paths.
rules.MF6: AIGP.md version field must match main.aisop.json version field. AIGP.md modules list must match actual .aisop.json files in directory. NODE COUNT VERIFICATION: for each module listed in AIGP.md modules[], if a 'nodes' field is present, verify it matches the actual functional node count in the corresponding .aisop.json file (functional nodes = total Mermaid nodes - Start - endNode). Mismatch is MF6-NODE-COUNT. Generate and ReviewStep MUST auto-compute and update AIGP.md node counts during AIGP.md write.
rules.MF7: Routing parameter symmetry: in Pattern B+ architectures, if existing sub-modules receive explicit routing parameters from the main router (e.g., operation_type for record, report_type for report), newly added sub-modules with internal routing decisions (ModeGate/TypeGate nodes) SHOULD receive an equivalent explicit routing parameter. This ensures deterministic routing and prevents duplicate NLU parsing in sub-modules. Check: for each sub-module with a ModeGate/routing decision node, verify it has a dedicated routing parameter passed from main, OR document the design choice to use user_request-based inference.
rules.MF8: Semantic operation consistency: within a module, operations of the same semantic type SHOULD route through the same node type. Specifically: all delete operations (expense delete, template delete, etc.) should converge to the module's delete node; all save/create/update operations should converge to the save node. When adding new paths, check existing paths with similar operation semantics and route through the same node types for architectural consistency. Check: identify operation semantics (create/read/update/delete) for each ModeGate route, verify same-type operations converge to same-type target nodes.
rules.MF9: Cross-module node invocation (bidirectional): for Pattern B+ architectures, verify BOTH directions of module connectivity. FORWARD CHECK: every parameter value main router passes to a sub-module must trigger a valid route in that sub-module's routing gate — if main passes operation='X' but sub-module has no route for 'X', it is MF9-FORWARD violation. REVERSE CHECK: every routing branch declared in a sub-module's routing gate (ModeGate, OperationGate, PhaseGate) must be reachable via at least one caller's delegation — if sub-module declares route 'Y' but no caller ever passes the parameter value that triggers 'Y', the route is a CROSS-MODULE ORPHAN (MF9-REVERSE violation). Check: for each sub-module, (1) identify all entry points from callers' delegation and trace parameter values passed, (2) for each sub-module routing node verify every branch has at least one triggering caller, (3) for each caller delegation verify the target route exists in sub-module. EXEMPTION: operations not triggered by standard main router delegation are exempt when annotated with their invocation context. Annotation taxonomy: '(user-initiated)' for operations invoked by direct user request (e.g., data deletion, export, manual crisis_check); '(event-triggered)' for operations invoked by runtime conditions rather than router dispatch (e.g., session-end summary, proactive recall triggered by session state); '(scheduled)' for operations invoked by time-based triggers (e.g., periodic decay, consolidation cron). Each annotation suppresses MF9-REVERSE warning for that route. Unannotated orphan routes remain violations.
rules.MF15: Output Contract Enforcement: every module output passed to the next pipeline stage MUST conform to its declared json_schema in STANDARD.data_contracts. Check: at each pipeline stage boundary (Research→Evolve, Generate→QualityGate, etc.), validate output against json_schema. Validation: (a) required fields present, (b) field types match, (c) enum constraints satisfied. Mode: WARNING by default; FAIL via quality_threshold='strict'. Contracts without json_schema are exempt from MF15.
rules.MF16: Tool Directory Consistency (Pattern G): verify (a) tool_dirs[].mcp_server references an existing mcp_server.json file, (b) mcp_server.json exposed_tools[].name entries have corresponding AIGP.md tools[] entries, (c) AIGP.md tools[] entries with mcp_server.server_id='file:///./mcp-tools' have corresponding mcp_server.json exposed_tools declarations, (d) if a2a-tools/agent_card.json exists, its aigp_extension.governance_hash must match AIGP.md governance_hash. N/A for programs without tool_dirs.

pipeline_rules.description: Full Pipeline execution rules for Create/Evolve operations.

rules.PL1: Create/Evolve MUST execute full pipeline: Research(structure)→Evolve→Generate→Research(quality)→Modify→Generate→QualityGate→Research(compliance)→Validate→Simulate→Review, and generate/update AIGP.md.
rules.PL2: Research is shared module called 3 times with different modes: structure (before Evolve), quality (before Modify), compliance (before Validate).
rules.PL3: QualityGate failure loops back to ModifyStep, max 2 retries then force pass with WARNING.
rules.PL5: Validate/Simulate/Explain/General are standalone operations — do NOT trigger pipeline.
rules.PL6: Pipeline context threading: old_version_context and pipeline_metadata are initialized by PipelineStart and accumulated across all pipeline steps. Each step appends its metrics to pipeline_metadata. ReviewStep receives both for summary generation.
rules.PL7: Output naming: Research module ALWAYS outputs as research_context (never compliance_context or structure_report). Use research_mode field to distinguish content.
rules.PL8: Incremental Pipeline: for Pattern B+ evolution, hash each sub-module's content before and after Generate. If a sub-module's hash is unchanged from old_version, downstream stages (Modify, Simulate detail traces) MAY skip that sub-module. Stage-skip decisions recorded in pipeline_metadata.stage_hashes.
rules.PL9: Quality Baseline: ReviewStep MUST persist quality_baseline to .version_history/{version}/quality_baseline.json on successful pipeline completion. PipelineStart MUST load previous baseline (if exists) and pass to SimulateStep for regression detection.
rules.PL10: Stage Output Validation (per stage): each Pipeline stage MUST validate its received inputs from prior stages before processing. Validation: (a) null check — if expected input is null/empty when it should contain data, log warning and trigger stage-specific fallback. (b) schema check — verify input matches STANDARD.data_contracts schema (e.g., research_context must have domain + verified_findings + verified_count). (c) retry policy — if validation fails, retry input retrieval once; if still failing, execute stage fallback (documented per stage). Fallbacks: Research output null → AI-knowledge with 'unverified' label; Modify input null → internal search; Validate/Simulate input null → inline execution with warning.
rules.PL11: AIGP.md Lifecycle: PipelineStart MUST verify AIGP.md exists in target directory. If missing (new creation), Generate1 creates AIGP.md with all required fields. If exists (evolution/modification), ReviewStep updates version, modules, quality fields after finalization. AIGP.md version MUST match final .aisop.json version.
rules.PL12: Pipeline Stage Enforcement Levels: MANDATORY — stage MUST execute, skip only on technical failure (file not found, invalid JSON), not on discretion. RECOMMENDED — stage SHOULD execute, skip requires explicit pipeline_metadata.skip_reason with justification. OPTIONAL — stage MAY be skipped freely. Stage classification: MANDATORY: PipelineStart, DependencyCheck, Generate1, ThreeDimTest, QualityGate, ReviewStep. RECOMMENDED: Research1, Research2, ModifyStep, Generate2, ValidateStep, SimulateStep. OPTIONAL: Research3, ObservabilityStep. Evolve mode override: SimulateStep escalates to MANDATORY for Evolve operations (evolution introduces higher regression risk). Skip tracking: any RECOMMENDED stage skipped → record in pipeline_metadata.skipped_stages[] with reason. ReviewStep MUST display skipped stages prominently with warning banner.
rules.PL19: Elicitation Protocol: Pipeline gates that require human input SHOULD emit standardized elicitation requests per STANDARD.data_contracts.elicitation. Applicable gates: (1) EvolveStep — LEVEL_A/B change presentation (type=form, fields per change item with accept/reject), (2) PostSimulateGate — RED issue handling (type=choice, options: auto-fix/proceed/abort), (3) AutoFixGate — auto-fix approval (type=confirm, default_action=approve), (4) QualityGate retry — retry/force-pass decision (type=choice). Elicitation is RECOMMENDED (not MANDATORY) — executors without structured UI support fall back to natural language presentation. When elicitation is used, include timeout_seconds and default_action for non-interactive pipeline runs.
rules.PL20: Pipeline Progress Protocol: Pipeline orchestrator SHOULD maintain a pipeline_task object per STANDARD.data_contracts.pipeline_task throughout pipeline execution. (a) INITIALIZATION: PipelineStart creates pipeline_task with task_id=pipeline_run_id, status='running', total_stages from pipeline configuration. (b) STAGE TRANSITION: at each stage boundary, update current_stage, append completed stage to progress.completed_stages, recalculate percentage. (c) ESTIMATION: if pipeline_metadata.stage_timings contains historical data, compute estimated_remaining_seconds from average durations of remaining stages. (d) EMISSION: progress updates are advisory — pipeline execution continues regardless of progress reporting success. (e) RECORDING: final pipeline_task state recorded in pipeline_metadata for observability.
rules.PL21: Pipeline Cancellation: Pipeline orchestrator SHOULD support cooperative cancellation via pipeline_task.cancel_requested flag. (a) CHECK FREQUENCY: check cancel_requested at each stage boundary (between stages, not mid-stage). Long-running stages (Research with multiple searches, Simulate with many scenarios) SHOULD also check at sub-step boundaries. (b) ON CANCEL: complete current stage if already in progress (do not interrupt mid-stage), save partial results to .version_history/ with status='cancelled' annotation, set pipeline_task.status='cancelled', report to user with list of completed stages and partial results location. (c) PARTIAL RESULTS: cancelled pipelines preserve all completed stage outputs — partial results are valid and can be resumed from. (d) COOPERATIVE: cancellation is cooperative, not preemptive — stages are never forcibly terminated. A stage may take up to its normal duration to honor cancellation.

data_contracts.description: Inter-module data schemas for Pipeline communication.

output_contract_enforcement.description: Every data contract SHOULD include json_schema (JSON Schema Draft 2020-12). MF15 validates module output against json_schema at pipeline stage boundaries.
output_contract_enforcement.validation_mode: WARNING by default; FAIL via quality_threshold='strict'
output_contract_enforcement.backward_compatibility: Contracts without json_schema exempt from MF15.

research_context.description: Output of Research module, consumed by Modify and Simulate. Field name is ALWAYS research_context regardless of mode — use research_mode field to distinguish.

schema.verified_findings: [{ finding: string, source_url: string, search_query: string }]
schema.best_practices: [string] — domain best practice statements (populated in quality mode, empty in others)
schema.compliance_notes: [{ standard: string, requirement: string, applicable: boolean }] — populated in compliance mode, empty in others
schema.structure_report: object — populated in structure mode only: { current_pattern, target_pattern, level_a_changes, level_b_changes, level_c_changes, search_verified_count }
schema.competitive_insights: array of {pattern: string, source_url: string}. Optional. Populated by QualityResearch competitive landscape step.
schema.domain_evolution_findings: array of {finding: string, source_url: string}. Optional. Populated by QualityResearch domain-specific search.

pipeline_metadata.description: Accumulated metadata passed through Pipeline stages for ReviewStep summary.

schema.schema_version: string — metadata schema version (default '1.0'). Readers MUST handle missing field as pre-13.2.0.
schema.simulate_quality_indicator: string — GREEN (0 red, <=2 yellow) | YELLOW (0 red, 3+ yellow) | RED (any red)
schema.pipeline_run_id: string — UUID v4 generated by PipelineStart for end-to-end traceability across all pipeline stages
schema.dependency_check_pass: boolean — true if all pre-flight checks passed
schema.dependency_check_warnings: number — count of non-blocking warnings from DependencyCheck
schema.modify_net_token_impact: number — net token change from Modify (positive = content added, negative = content removed, zero = restructure only)
schema.stage_timings: object|null — { stage_name: { start: ISO8601, end: ISO8601, duration_ms: number } } — Pipeline performance telemetry per stage
schema.stage_hashes: object|null — { stage_name: string } — Content hash of each stage's output for incremental skip detection
schema.quality_gate_failure_category: string|null — cognitive|intrinsic|detail|null — Lowest-scoring dimension when FAIL
schema.summary_consistency_check: string — PASS/FAIL — summary matches current capabilities
schema.post_simulate_gate_result: string|null — PASS|BLOCKED|WARN — result of PL13 Post-Simulate Gate
schema.post_simulate_gate_red_issues: [string]|null — list of RED issues that triggered gate block
schema.token_tracking: object|null — { stage_name: estimated_tokens } — per-stage token consumption estimates for PL15 enforcement
schema.token_budget_utilization: number|null — current total estimated tokens / runtime.token_budget * 100
schema.qrg_results: object|null — { qrg1: PASS|YELLOW, qrg2: PASS|RED|N/A, qrg3: PASS|RED, qrg4: PASS|YELLOW|N/A, qrg5: PASS|RED } — Quality Regression Guard evaluation results (QRG5 Security Floor: I2/I8 <=2 → RED)
schema.cache_hits: [{ stage: string, cache_key: string, cache_age_seconds: number }] — stages that used cached results (per PL17)
schema.cache_misses: [string] — stages that executed fresh (no valid cache)
schema.creation_experience_written: boolean — whether creation_experience was persisted at pipeline end

test_report.description: Output of Generate ThreeDimTest, consumed by QualityGate and Review.

schema.dim_grades: { cognitive: string (A/B/C/D), intrinsic: string (A/B/C/D), detail: string (A/B/C/D) }
schema.grade_thresholds: S(>=4.50), A(>=3.50), B(>=2.50), C(>=1.50), D(<1.50). Per-dimension grades use same thresholds mapped to A/B/C/D (no per-dimension S).
schema.dim_scores: { cognitive: number (1-5), intrinsic: number (1-5), detail: number (1-5) }
schema.per_item_scoring: Each check item (C1-C7, I1-I13, D1-D10) scored individually 1-5: 5=Full compliance (exemplary), 4=Minor issue (cosmetic/documentation), 3=Moderate issue (functional but improvable), 2=Significant issue (partially non-compliant), 1=Critical issue (missing/fundamentally broken). Dimension score = average of applicable item scores.
schema.issue_count: { cognitive: number, intrinsic: number, detail: number }
schema.per_file_grades: object|null — for multi-file Pattern C/D: per-file grade breakdown
schema.automated_results: lint_report -- machine-verifiable results from automated checks

traffic_report.description: Output of Generate TrafficClassify, consumed by Review.

schema.green: [{ item: string, action: string }] — auto-applied silently
schema.yellow: [{ item: string, action: string }] — auto-applied + reported
schema.red: [{ item: string, reason: string, proposed_change: string }] — requires human approval
schema.dim_grades: { cognitive: string, intrinsic: string, detail: string }

evolution_context.description: Old version context threaded through Pipeline for evolution verification and comparative testing.

schema.multi_file: boolean — true if target AISOP has multiple .aisop.json files (Pattern B+)

evolution_context.consumers: Generate (evolution verification), Modify (suggestion weighting), Simulate (comparative scenarios Category G)

quality_baseline.description: Persisted quality scores and simulation results from previous successful pipeline run. Used for regression detection.

schema.schema_version: string — schema format version (default '1.0'). Readers MUST handle missing field as pre-13.2.0 format with null defaults.
schema.version: string — version that produced this baseline
schema.weighted_score: number — ThreeDimTest weighted score at baseline
schema.dim_scores: { cognitive: number, intrinsic: number, detail: number }

quality_baseline.consumers: PipelineStart (loads previous baseline), SimulateStep (regression comparison), ReviewStep (trend display)

telemetry_report.schema: { pipeline_run_id: string, bottleneck_stage: string, stage_time_distribution: object, timing_anomalies: [{stage, expected_ms, actual_ms}], score_trajectory: string (improving|stable|declining), dimension_trends: object, persistent_bottlenecks: [string], quality_regression_signal: boolean, historical_runs_analyzed: number, recommendations: [string] }

agent_card.description: A2A Agent Card configuration for AISOP interoperability.
agent_card.schema: { name: string, version: string, capabilities: [{name, description, input_schema, output_schema}], supportedInterfaces: [string], authentication: object, url: string }

validation_report.description: Output of ValidateStep, consumed by SimulateStep and ReviewStep.

schema.issues: [{ id: string, severity: string (WARNING|FAIL), description: string, location: string }]

simulation_report.description: Output of SimulateStep, consumed by ReviewStep.

schema.path_coverage: string — percentage of reachable paths traced
schema.scenario_matrix: [{ category: string (A-M), total: number, pass: number, yellow: number, red: number }]
schema.regression_flags: [{ metric: string, baseline_value: string, current_value: string, delta: string }]
schema.issues: [{ id: string, severity: string (YELLOW|RED), description: string, location: string }]
schema.remediation: string — root cause analysis and fix guidance (generated by YellowRemediationGuide for YELLOW items)

vision_context.description: Structured capture of program vision and design intent, produced by EvolveStep or user input, consumed by Generate and Review.

schema.purpose: string — what problem the program solves
schema.target_users: string — who will use this program
schema.differentiation: string — what makes this different from alternatives
schema.vision_completeness: number (0-1) — how fully the vision has been captured

vision_context.produced_by: main.aisop.json EvolveStep (from user input or Research1)

ecosystem_map.description: Map of all AISOP programs in a Pattern F ecosystem, produced by advisor.aisop.json AnalyzeScope, consumed by Generate for cross-program consistency.

schema.programs: [{ id: string, pattern: string, tools: [string], summary: string }]
schema.integration_points: [{ from_id: string, to_id: string, contract: string }]

reflection_notes.description: Stage-by-stage reflections and observations collected during pipeline execution, consumed by Review for meta-analysis.

schema.reflections: [{ stage: string, observation: string, recommendation: string }]

applicability_condition.description: Structured preconditions for AIGP program activation. Completes the Skill Quadruple S=(C,π,T,R) where C = applicability_condition.

schema.triggers: [string] — input patterns or events that activate this program (e.g., 'user asks to create an AIGP program', 'file with .aisop.json extension detected')
schema.preconditions: [string] — conditions that must be true before activation (e.g., 'AIGP_Standard.core.aisop.json accessible', 'workspace_dir writable')
schema.exclusions: [string] — conditions that prevent activation even if triggers match (e.g., 'input is not AISOP format', 'target is already at latest version')
schema.confidence_threshold: number (0.0-1.0) — minimum confidence for automatic activation, default 0.8

applicability_condition.relationship_to_intent_examples: intent_examples remain as human-readable activation examples. applicability_condition provides machine-parseable activation rules. Both coexist in AIGP.md.
applicability_condition.enforcement: D10 checks applicability_condition presence and non-empty triggers.

token_estimate.description: Per-stage token consumption estimates for budget tracking.
token_estimate.aggregation: Pipeline-level: sum all stage estimates. Compare against AIGP.md runtime.token_budget.

mcp_config.description: MCP server configuration for tool exposure.
mcp_config.schema: { server_name: string, transport: string (stdio|streamable-http), tools: [{name, description, input_schema}], auth: object, config_stub: object }

mcp_tool_mapping.description: Mapping between AIGP program tools and MCP Server exposure. Produced by advisor mcp_adapter sub-graph.

schema.server_name: string — MCP Server name (derived from AIGP program name)
schema.tools: [{ name: string, description: string, inputSchema: object, annotations: { readOnlyHint: boolean, destructiveHint: boolean, idempotentHint: boolean, openWorldHint: boolean } }]
schema.aigp_source: { program_id: string, version: string, trust_level: number, governance_hash: string }
schema.capability_summary: [string] — list of exposed capabilities for registry integration

discovery_result.description: Output of DiscoverStep — ranked list of matching AIGP programs in workspace.

schema.matches: [{ name: string, version: string, status: string, summary: string, relevance_score: number, match_reason: string, path: string }]

registry.description: Federated registry for AIGP program discovery. Supports local workspace scan and optional remote registry query.

schema.entries: [{ name: string, version: string, author: string, quality_grade: string (S/A/B/C/D), weighted_score: number, tags: [string], uri: string, pattern: string (A-F), trust_level: number (1-4), summary: string, status: string (active|deprecated|archived), governance_hash: string|null, mcp_capabilities: [string]|null, last_updated: string (ISO 8601), source: string (LOCAL|REMOTE) }]

registry_metadata.remote_available: boolean — whether remote registry was reachable
registry_metadata.auth_method: string|null — authentication method used: 'bearer_token'|'api_key'|null. If null, registry is public (no auth required).

migration_guide.description: Deprecation migration document generated by DeprecateFlow.

skill_md.description: SKILL.md bidirectional converter data contracts for interoperability with 30+ Agent Skills platforms.

export_schema.name: string — SKILL.md skill name
export_schema.author: string — skill author
export_schema.instructions: string — behavioral instructions (prose condensed from AISOP functions)
export_schema.input_schema: object — JSON Schema converted from AIGP parameters
export_schema.metadata: object — AIGP governance extension (protocol, governance_hash, trust_level, quality_score, pattern, axiom_0)

skill_md.consumed_by: External Agent Skills platforms (export) / AIGP pipeline (import)

pipeline_cache.description: Cache entry for reusable pipeline stage outputs (per PL17).

schema.result: object — cached stage output (research_context or validation_report)
schema.target_content_hash: string — SHA-256 of target AISOP content at cache time
schema.ttl_seconds: number — time-to-live (default: 86400 for Research, 3600 for Validate)
schema.cache_hit_count: number — times this cache entry was used
schema.effective_ttl_seconds: number — actual TTL used for this cache entry (may differ from base due to dynamic adjustment)

cache_policy.description: Dynamic cache TTL policy for pipeline stage caching (extends PL17).

schema.base_ttl_seconds: number — static TTL before dynamic adjustment
schema.dynamic_ttl_seconds: number|null — computed adaptive TTL (null if insufficient history)

creation_experience.description: Pipeline learning record capturing challenges, solutions, and quality outcomes for future reference. Enables learning loop across pipeline runs.

schema.schema_version: string — schema format version (default '1.0'). Readers MUST handle missing field as pre-13.2.0 format with null defaults.
schema.challenges: [{ stage: string, issue: string, resolution: string }] — problems encountered during pipeline
schema.quality_outcome: { weighted_score: number, grade: string, dimension_scores: { cognitive: number, intrinsic: number, detail: number } }
schema.simulation_highlights: { scenario_coverage: string, dead_nodes: number, red_issues: number }
schema.research_insights: [{ finding: string, applied: boolean }] — most impactful research findings

intent_vectors.description: Optional pre-computed semantic embedding vectors for NLU intent routing. Enables vector-similarity matching alongside keyword-based NLU classification.

schema.similarity_threshold: number — minimum cosine similarity for auto-routing (default: 0.85)

intent_vectors.note: Requires external embedding model. When unavailable, NLU falls back to keyword+LLM classification (existing behavior). This is an optional acceleration, not a requirement.

program_identity.description: Verifiable program identity for provenance and supply chain integrity.

schema.program_id: string — globally unique identifier using reverse-DNS format (e.g., 'aigp.dev/aigp_creator')
schema.signature: string|null — Ed25519 signature of governance_hash (optional, enables cryptographic verification)
schema.signature_public_key: string|null — Ed25519 public key for signature verification
schema.registry_url: string|null — URL in AIGP registry for discovery

program_identity.verification: I11 checks: (1) program_id format valid (reverse-DNS), (2) publisher is non-empty, (3) if signature present, verify against governance_hash using signature_public_key, (4) if registry_url present, verify URL format.
program_identity.relationship: Extends governance_hash (PL14) with active identity verification. governance_hash verifies content integrity; program_identity verifies publisher provenance.

elicitation.description: Standardized human-in-the-loop interaction format for Pipeline gates and decision points.

schema.context: string|null — additional context for informed decision-making
schema.options: [{id: string, label: string, description: string}]|null — for choice type only
schema.fields: [{name: string, type: string, required: boolean, default: any}]|null — for form type only
schema.timeout_seconds: number|null — auto-proceed after timeout with default_action
schema.default_action: string|null — action taken on timeout or user skip

schema.modified_changes: { change_id: { scope_override: string, rationale: string } } — user-modified changes with overrides
schema.dependency_acknowledged: boolean — user has reviewed dependency matrix
schema.user_notes: string|null — free-form user comments on evolution decisions

evolution_decision.defaults: All LEVEL_B changes approved, all LEVEL_C auto-applied.
evolution_decision.timeout_behavior: If no response within timeout_seconds, apply default_action (approve all LEVEL_B).

modes.form: Structured data collection with typed fields. Use for EvolveStep LEVEL_A/B change presentation where user provides per-item accept/reject decisions.
modes.choice: Selection from predefined options. Use for PostSimulateGate RED handling (auto-fix / proceed / abort) and QualityGate retry decisions.
modes.confirm: Binary yes/no approval. Use for AutoFixGate auto-fix approval and simple pipeline gate decisions.

elicitation.enforcement: PL19 defines which pipeline gates SHOULD use elicitation format.
elicitation.backward_compatibility: If executor does not support structured elicitation, fall back to natural language presentation (existing behavior). Elicitation is advisory — pipeline execution is never blocked by elicitation format support.

pipeline_task.description: Async pipeline task representation for progress tracking, cooperative cancellation, and optional external event emission.

schema.task_id: string — UUID v4, same as pipeline_run_id from pipeline_metadata
schema.status: string — pending | running | paused | blocked | completed | cancelled | failed
schema.estimated_remaining_seconds: number|null — computed from stage_timings historical averages
schema.cancel_requested: boolean — set to true to request cooperative cancellation
schema.effective_trust_level: number|null — runtime trust level (1-4). Defaults to AIGP.md trust_level.level at pipeline start. Executor MAY downgrade during execution (e.g., T4->T3). Gate stages (QualityGate, PostSimulateGate) MUST check: if effective_trust_level < declared level, apply stricter thresholds. Cannot increase above declared level.
schema.event_hooks: object|null — optional external event emission configuration (see event_hooks_schema)

event_hooks_schema.description: EVENT EMISSION (optional): pipeline_task MAY include event_hooks for external system integration. Pipelines without event_hooks configured operate identically to v13.0.0.

schema.on_gate_pass: { emit_target: 'webhook'|'stdout'|'file', payload_template: { gate: string, result: 'PASS', score: number, timestamp: ISO_8601 } }
schema.on_gate_fail: { emit_target: 'webhook'|'stdout'|'file', payload_template: { stage: string, reason: string, severity: string } }
schema.on_gate_warn: { emit_target: 'webhook'|'stdout'|'file', payload_template: { stage: string, details: string } }
schema.on_complete: { emit_target: 'webhook'|'stdout'|'file', payload_template: { version: string, quality_score: number, duration_seconds: number } }

event_hooks_schema.emission_points: QualityGate (PASS -> on_gate_pass, FAIL -> on_gate_fail), PostSimulateGate (GREEN -> on_gate_pass, YELLOW -> on_gate_warn, BLOCKED -> on_gate_fail), ReviewStep completion (on_complete).
event_hooks_schema.transport_note: Event transport implementations are executor-specific — STANDARD defines the schema, not the transport.

state_machine.transitions: pending→running (pipeline starts), running→paused (user pause), paused→running (user resume), running→blocked (STANDARD integrity BLOCK — core infrastructure missing), running→completed (pipeline success), running→cancelled (cancel_requested honored at stage boundary), running→failed (unrecoverable error)
state_machine.terminal_states: completed, cancelled, failed, blocked — no further transitions allowed
state_machine.trust_downgrade: effective_trust_level can decrease (never increase above declared) at any stage boundary. Immediate effect on subsequent stages. Record: pipeline_metadata.trust_downgrade_events = [{ from_level, to_level, at_stage, reason }].

pipeline_task.consumers: UI layers, monitoring dashboards, pipeline orchestrator, observability module, external systems (via event_hooks)

capability_negotiation.description: Runtime capability negotiation between AIGP programs. Programs declare offered and required capabilities; DependencyCheck computes negotiation results to determine interoperability.

schema.required: [string] — capabilities this program needs from dependencies (e.g., 'file_read')
schema.negotiation_result: string — 'full' (all required met) | 'partial' (some met, WARNING) | 'incompatible' (critical unmet, YELLOW)
schema.negotiated: [string] — intersection of offered and required that matched

self_validation.description: Pre-negotiation self-check: verify program's own capabilities.offered are supportable by its tools[] and capabilities.required are satisfiable by its tools[] or declared dependencies. Run before external dependency negotiation.

schema.offered_valid: boolean — true if all offered capabilities have supporting tools
schema.required_valid: boolean — true if all required capabilities have satisfying tools or dependencies
schema.unsupported_offered: [string] — offered capabilities with no supporting tool
schema.unsatisfied_required: [string] — required capabilities with no satisfying tool or dependency

capability_negotiation.consumed_by: Pipeline context (interoperability decisions), K5 simulation scenarios

sampling_request.description: LLM sampling protocol for modules that need additional LLM inference during pipeline execution. Controlled by PL23 with token budget, rate limiting, prompt sanitization, and audit trail.

schema.requesting_module: string — module id that initiated sampling
schema.prompt: string — sanitized prompt text sent to LLM
schema.max_tokens: number — maximum tokens, must be <= 20% of PL15 pipeline token_budget
schema.context: object|null — structured context passed to sampling

audit.prompt_sanitized: boolean — whether C2 guard was applied
audit.injection_detected: boolean — whether injection patterns found and neutralized
audit.budget_remaining_pct: number — remaining token budget percentage after this request
audit.request_sequence: number — 1-based sequence within pipeline run (max 5 per PL23)

schema.fallback_result: string|null — hardcoded decision result if sampling failed

sampling_request.produced_by: Any module invoking LLM sampling during pipeline execution

aigp_package.description: AIGP packaging format for distributing programs as self-contained .aigp archives (ZIP with manifest.json). Enables registry publishing, integrity verification, and governance hash validation.

schema.files: [{ path: string, sha256: string, size_bytes: number }] — all files with integrity hashes

ui_output.description: UI rendering instructions for AIGP program dashboards, forms, and visualizations.

schema.components: array — [{type, title, data_source, config}] UI component declarations
schema.interactivity: boolean — true if components accept user input (forms)

auto_fix_proposal.description: Automated fix proposal generated from ThreeDimTest/Simulation issues.

schema.proposals: [{ issue_id: string, severity: string (RED/YELLOW/ADVISORY), category: string (format/consistency/metadata/reference), file_path: string, current_content: string, proposed_content: string, impact_scope: [string], confidence: string (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), auto_applicable: boolean, status: string (pending/approved/applied/rejected) }]
schema.summary: { total: number, auto_applicable: number, manual_required: number, applied: number }
schema.snapshot_id: string — pre-fix file snapshot ID for rollback

lint_report.description: Automated verification results for objectively testable ThreeDimTest check items. Separates machine-verifiable results from AI subjective scores.

schema.automated_checks: [{ check_id: string (D2/D4/D6/I7/MF1/MF6/MF15/PL14/I13a/I13b), check_name: string, result: string (PASS/FAIL/WARNING), detail: string, evidence: string }]
schema.summary: { total_automated: number, passed: number, failed: number, coverage_percentage: number -- automated / total ThreeDimTest items }
schema.subjective_items: [string] -- items that remain AI-judged (e.g., C1, C3, C6, I5, D1, D5)

tool_dir_manifest.description: Tool directory manifest for Pattern G programs. Describes bundled tool implementations, MCP server configuration, file integrity hashes, and A2A agent card. Produced during GenerateToolDirs and verified by Code Trust Gate during pack/unpack.

schema.tool_dirs: [{ type: string (python-tools|ts-tools|go-tools|rust-tools|shell-tools|mcp-tools|a2a-tools|agentskills-tools|n8n-tools|other-tools), runtime: string, entry_point: string, file_count: number }]
schema.mcp_server: { schema_version: string, server_id: string, transport: 'stdio', runtime: string, entry_point: string, exposed_tools: [{ name: string, description: string }], file_hashes: { [filename]: sha256_hex }, governance: { aigp_protocol: string, trust_level: string, governance_hash: string } }
schema.agent_card: { name: string, description: string, capabilities: [string], endpoint: string } | null (optional, A2A v1.0)

tool_dir_manifest.consumed_by: review.aisop.json (I13/MF16 validation), simulate.aisop.json (Category M testing), advisor.aisop.json (package Code Trust Gate)

directory_layout.{version}/main.aisop.json: Copy of the AISOP file(s) at this version
directory_layout.{version}/quality_baseline.json: Quality scores and simulation coverage from this version's pipeline run

meta_schema.grades: { cognitive: number, intrinsic: number, detail: number }
meta_schema.pipeline_metadata: object|null — full pipeline_metadata from the pipeline run that produced this version

simulation_categories.description: Standardized test scenario categories for comprehensive AISOP simulation. Categories F-L defined in AIGP_Standard.ecosystem.aisop.json.

categories.A_core_intents: Test each NLU intent with representative input. One scenario per intent minimum.
categories.B_boundary_rules: Test each NLU boundary rule with ambiguous input that could match multiple intents. One scenario per rule.
categories.C_multi_step_workflows: Test chains of operations requiring state persistence or sequential interaction (e.g., search→save→compare).
categories.D_error_paths: Test each Error fallback by simulating tool failures (search fails, file read fails, write fails).
categories.E_edge_cases: Test input limits (>max length, empty, injection, path traversal, mixed language, emoji), output limits (>max items), and guard triggers. SUB-CATEGORIES: see AIGP_Standard.performance.aisop.json for E.figurative and E.injection sub-category definitions.

simulation_categories.state_transition_applicability: Category I applies only to stateful patterns (Pattern E, or any pattern with file_system write side_effects). For stateless patterns, Category I scenario count is 0 and does not affect coverage formula.
simulation_categories.trust_boundary_applicability: Category J applies to programs with trust_level >= T3 (supervised or autonomous). For T1/T2 programs, Category J scenario count is 0 and does not affect coverage formula.
simulation_categories.multi_agent_applicability: Category K applies to programs with ecosystem_map in AIGP.md OR non-empty dependencies[] in AIGP.md. Standalone programs without ecosystem or dependency declarations: Category K scenario count is 0, does not affect coverage formula.

evolution_rules.description: EvolveFlow uses LEVEL_A/B/C classification instead of ratio-based limits. Separates structural evolution from quality improvement.

LEVEL_A.name: 用户决定 (Structural)
LEVEL_A.scope: Pattern upgrade/downgrade, module add/remove, tool set changes, statefulness changes (stateless→session→persistent).
LEVEL_A.decision: MUST have explicit user confirmation. Never auto-apply.

LEVEL_B.name: 引擎建议 (Functional)
LEVEL_B.scope: New function nodes, NLU intent add/remove, cross-module data flow changes, high-impact enhancements.
LEVEL_B.decision: Engine recommends with importance score (1-5). User can accept/reject/override.

LEVEL_C.name: 引擎自决 (Incremental)
LEVEL_C.scope: New steps within existing functions, boundary rule improvements, search strategy optimization, formatting.
LEVEL_C.decision: Auto-apply. User notified but no confirmation required.

constraints.max_changes_per_evolution: No hard cap. Recommend <=15 for manageability.
constraints.separation: EvolveFlow handles structural/functional changes only. Quality improvements (I2 guards, I4 circuit-breakers, D4 naming) belong to ModifyFlow.

version_management.description: Unified version bump rules for all pipeline operations.

rules.modify_only: patch+1 — quality fixes without functional changes
rules.evolve_level_b: minor+1 — functional changes without structural changes
rules.evolve_level_a: major+1 — structural changes (pattern upgrade, module add/remove, tool set changes)
rules.mixed: highest applicable level wins (A > B > C)
rules.reset_rule: Per semantic versioning: minor+1 resets patch to 0 (e.g., 1.0.2 → 1.1.0 not 1.1.2); major+1 resets minor and patch to 0 (e.g., 1.2.3 → 2.0.0 not 2.2.3)

version_management.enforcement: Generate ApplyFixes and main ReviewStep MUST reference these rules for version computation. No inline version logic — always delegate to STANDARD.evolution_rules.version_management.

aisop.main:
graph TD
  Start[Start Node] --> Check{Decision?}
  Check --> |Yes| SubTask[Call Sub-Graph]
  Check --> |No| AtomicTool[Call Function]
  SubTask --> endNode((End))
  AtomicTool --> endNode

Start.step1: Parse input. Validate: type guard, injection guard, path guard, size guard, encoding guard.
Start.Error: Invalid -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: show input format guide -> inform.

Check.step1: Classify input into intent A or intent B. CONFIDENCE: <0.80 -> present both options -> ask user.
Check.constraints: Boundary rule: intent A when X present; intent B when Y present.
Check.Error: Unclassifiable -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: present binary choice -> inform.

AtomicTool.step1: Execute tool action. Atomic write: .tmp rename + .bak backup.
AtomicTool.Error: Fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: display result as inline text -> inform.

SubTask.Error: Sub-graph execution fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: inline sub-graph steps as direct execution -> inform.
